frameworks.name: test-retest reliability
frameworks.one_line: Multi-dimensional replicability framework for task-based fMRI activation maps.

frameworks.name: statistical power analysis
frameworks.one_line: Sample-size effects on detection in neuroimaging studies.

core_question: How does sample size affect the empirical replicability of task-based fMRI results across spatial scales?
key_assumptions: replicability is decomposable into reliability, coherence, and stability
independent subsamples from a large cohort approximate independent replications
voxelwise and clusterwise inference characterize complementary spatial scales
manipulations: sample size of independent subsamples drawn from 1400 IMAGEN subjects
spatial resolution of replicability assessment (voxelwise vs clusters)
key_findings: larger sample sizes yield higher replicability across all three measures
voxelwise and small-area replicability requires samples larger than typical fMRI studies
clusterwise activation replicates more readily than voxelwise activation
interpretations: there is a trade-off between spatial resolution and replicability in task fMRI
typical fMRI sample sizes are insufficient for voxel-level replicable inference
